title: TC001-Run a session end-to-end from preview to completion
description: Verifies a user can generate a queue preview, start the session, observe step progression, and reach a completed session state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Session completed')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The session should show a completion event after the session finishes"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The queue/session feature is not present on the current page, so the task cannot be executed.

Observations:
- The page shows the default Vite starter content (logo, "Get started", "Count is 0").
- No UI controls, buttons, or links for 'queue preview', 'start session', 'advance step', or session status were found in the interactive elements.
- Only a generic count button and external links are available; there are no session-related controls.

title: TC002-Pause and resume without losing timing continuity
description: Verifies pausing stops ticks and resuming continues timing from a corrected elapsed/remaining time without drift or jumps.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Session resumed')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The UI should show Session resumed after resuming the session.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Elapsed')]").nth(0).is_visible() and await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Remaining')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The elapsed and remaining timers should be visible and continue updating after resuming the session."]}
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The session pause/resume timing feature required for this test is not present, so the verification could not be performed.

Observations:
- The app root at http://localhost:5173 displays the Vite starter page content rather than a session/timer UI.
- No interactive controls labeled Start, Pause, or Resume (or similar session controls) were present in the page's interactive elements.

title: TC003-Start session from preview and consume the same ordered queue
description: Verifies starting from the preview runs the session in the same step order as shown in the preview list.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert (await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Step 1')]").nth(0).is_visible()) and (await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Step 2')]").nth(0).is_visible()), "The running session should display Step 1 followed by Step 2 in the same order as shown in the preview."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature could not be reached — the application root shows the default Vite starter page and no queue preview/session controls are present.

Observations:
- The page displays the Vite starter content instead of a preview or session UI.
- No buttons or links labeled 'preview', 'queue', 'request preview', or 'start session' were found among the interactive elements.
- The visible interactive elements are Vite-specific links and a generic counter button ("Count is 0").

title: TC004-Advance mid-step starts the next step immediately
description: Verifies advancing during an active step ends the current step and immediately starts the next step with timing reset for the new step.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Step started')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "A step start event for the newly active step should be visible after advancing.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '00:00')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The elapsed time for the newly active step should be near the beginning (showing 00:00) after advancing."]} PMID_DUP_REMOVE.
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The UI does not provide controls to start or advance a session/step, so the requested behavior cannot be tested.

Observations:
- No buttons or controls labeled 'start', 'start session', 'advance', 'step', 'timer', or 'elapsed time' were found on the page.
- The only potentially related state text is 'Count is 0', which is not labeled or presented as a step timer or step indicator.

title: TC005-Generate and display an ordered preview list with step metadata
description: Verifies the smart queue generator produces an ordered list and the preview renders step labels, types, and durations.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '1.')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "An ordered list of queued steps should be visible in the preview after requesting a queue preview"
        assert 'Label' in await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Step 1')]").nth(0).text_content() and 'Type' in await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Step 1')]").nth(0).text_content() and 'Duration' in await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Step 1')]").nth(0).text_content(), "Each listed step should display a label, type, and duration in the preview"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The smart queue generator feature was not found on the application's homepage, so its ordered list and preview cannot be verified.

Observations:
- The page displays the default Vite starter content (Get started, Count is 0).
- No UI elements or links labeled 'queue', 'smart queue', 'queue preview', or 'generator' were present.
- Interactive elements are limited to Vite documentation/community links and unrelated buttons.

title: TC006-Show empty state when starting with no queued steps
description: Verifies attempting to start without any queued steps shows an empty state and no session begins.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'There are no steps to run')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The UI should show an empty state message indicating there are no steps to run.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Session not running')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The session should remain not running because there were no queued steps to start.",
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The application does not expose a 'start session' control or an empty-state message for when no queued steps exist, so the test cannot verify the expected behavior.

Observations:
- The page shows the Vite starter template content ('Get started', 'Edit src/App.tsx', 'Count is 0') rather than an app UI with session controls.
- No 'Start', 'Run', or session-related button or control is present in the interactive elements.
- There is no visible empty-state message indicating 'no steps to run' or similar.

title: TC007-Conditional blocks evaluate at runtime and affect realized next steps
description: Verifies conditional evaluation occurs at step execution time and changes the realized step sequence shown during playback.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Conditional evaluation: true')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The conditional evaluation outcome should be shown when the conditional step starts.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Realized step sequence: Branch A -> Step 3')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The subsequent realized step sequence should reflect the conditional outcome after evaluation.",
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature could not be reached — the application loaded is the default Vite starter and does not expose the queue preview or conditional playback controls required for this test.

Observations:
- The page shows the default Vite welcome content ('Get started', 'Count is 0').
- There are no UI elements or links referencing 'queue preview', 'conditional', 'session', or 'playback'.

title: TC008-Preview marks conditional steps as deferred evaluation
description: Verifies when the preview includes conditional blocks, the preview indicates those steps are deferred for runtime evaluation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Deferred for runtime evaluation')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The preview should mark conditional steps as deferred for runtime evaluation."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST BLOCKED
